tagline: autoresearch collects five PRs while the job market visualizer empire sprawls across continents
editionNote: Five pending PRs on autoresearch, five repos quietly accumulating commits, and a licensing question that nobody answered.

articles.headline: autoresearch patches GPU compatibility gap with Turing fallback
articles.deck: A contributor adds capability-aware flash attention switching to run training on cheaper cards.
articles.body: #268 from Nidhicodes adds Turing GPU (T4/RTX) compatibility via capability-aware FA3 fallback with fp16 and GradScaler support. The change addresses a real friction point: not everyone has H100s. Meanwhile, #265 saves checkpoints before eval to survive crashes, and #264 guards against zero grad accumulation steps.
articles.illustrationPrompt: GPU chip with cascading fallback arrows, one path glowing brighter than others

articles.headline: autoresearch waits on docs and bug fixes in a five-PR stack
articles.deck: Community contributors chip at progress plots, gradients, and contribution guidelines.
articles.body: #263 fixes undefined 'best' variable in progress plots. #258 proposes a minimal CONTRIBUTING.md focused on research context. The PR queue suggests active tinkering but no merges this week.
articles.illustrationPrompt: scattered papers with checkmarks and X marks, one highlighted in red

articles.headline: jobs repo now exists in at least five regional versions
articles.deck: The original BLS treemap spawned India, UK, and EU variants as the job market visualization empire quietly expands.
articles.body: @karpathy's jobs project has fractured into specialized regional instances: india-jobs-ai-exposure for NCO occupation groups, jobs-uk for ONS data, and AI-ISCO for EU ESCO standards. Each repo shows five commits this week. An open issue credits an Indian adaptation built on the original concept. The archive fork karpathy-jobs-archive preserves the original before deletion.
articles.illustrationPrompt: treemap visualization splitting into multiple colored sections, each with different symbols

articles.headline: autoresearch wants a licensing policy and a judge layer
articles.deck: The open issues reveal ambitions: pluggable optimization tracks, snapshot-diff scoring, and data-centric research.
articles.body: Someone asked whether autoresearch code can be reused without a license and got no answer. The backlog includes Auto-Research Tracks (pluggable idle optimization), a JudgeModel layer for snapshot-diff scoring and binary rubric verification, and expanded harness generation for pattern and security exploration. No timelines yet.
articles.illustrationPrompt: flowchart with multiple branching paths, some paths converging into a central node

closingNote: Five repos, five commits each, five open questions nobody asked for.
